body:
## Problem

The read path is documented to feed the shared repair queue when it excludes a checksum-failing fragment. That works when the store returns corrupt bytes and `read.rs` decodes them locally, but real verifying stores return `IntegrityFault` instead of returning corrupt bytes.

`read.rs` currently does not classify those errors as corruption findings:

- `EcScheme::None`: `get_fragment_at(...).await?` returns before `corrupt.push(chunk.id)` can run.
- `EcScheme::ReedSolomon`: the loop only handles `if let Ok(Some(fragment)) = fetched`; all `Err` values, including `IntegrityFault`, are ignored as absent shards.

## Evidence

- `crates/chunkstore-fs/src/lib.rs`: `get_fragment()` verifies on read and maps verification failures to `IntegrityFault`; the comment says this lets scrub/read record repair obligations.
- `crates/chunkstore-grpc/src/client.rs`: gRPC `DATA_LOSS` is mapped back to `IntegrityFault` for the same seam contract.
- `crates/core/src/read.rs`: corruption is pushed only after local `decode(&fragment)` failure or misplaced decoded header.
- `crates/core/tests/read_repair.rs`: the regression tests use an in-memory store that returns raw corrupt bytes, so they do not cover the real `FsChunkStore`/gRPC behavior.
- `crates/custodian/src/scrub.rs`: scrub already handles both paths correctly: corrupt bytes and `Err(e) if is_integrity_fault(e.as_ref())`.

## Impact

Read-time repair is silently skipped for corrupt fragments discovered through the real disk and gRPC backends. EC reads may still succeed from other shards, but the durable repair obligation is not created. Single-fragment reads can fail without leaving the promised repair queue entry.

## Suggested fix

Mirror scrub's classifier in `read.rs`:

- Treat `IntegrityFault` from `get_fragment_at` as a corruption finding and push the chunk id.
- Continue/read around it for Reed-Solomon.
- For `EcScheme::None`, enqueue before surfacing the error.

Add tests using a store that returns `IntegrityFault` from `get_fragment`/`get_fragment_at`, plus ideally an integration regression with `FsChunkStore` or the gRPC client.

milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: Read repair should enqueue IntegrityFaults from verifying stores
